title.en: Australia news live: Brittany Higgins accuses Hanson of victim-blaming in ‘atrocious’ comments on domestic violence
title.zh: 澳大利亚新闻直播：布列塔尼·希金斯指责汉森就家庭暴力发表“恶劣”言论是受害者有罪论

summary.en: Brittany Higgins has accused Pauline Hanson of victim-blaming following comments made about domestic violence, sparking public debate.
summary.zh: 布列塔尼·希金斯指责宝琳·汉森就家庭暴力发表的言论是“受害者有罪论”，引发了公众辩论。

body.en: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales premier, Chris Minns , says the state’s Labor party is under threat from One Nation and “we’ve got to fight for those votes”. Appearing on ABC Radio National this morning, Minns was asked if One Nation was a “racist party”. He said people who had concerns about the levels of immigration weren’t necessarily racist but some of the things Pauline Hanson had said were. Certainly some of the things Pauline Hanson says are definitely racist, when she said there’s no such thing as a good Muslim. I mean, how else do you characterise that? You’ve got to be straight with people. And that clearly is. But I don’t think everyone who’s supporting them currently is racist at all … And we’ve got to fight for those votes. I think that we are under threat in a whole bunch of seats as a result of the surge of votes for One Nation … So we’re doing some what we regard as practical commonsense changes to policy in New South Wales. When people join the Do Not Call Register or unsubscribe from marketing messages, they are making a clear choice. Those choices must be respected – especially given the heightened risks of financial loss and psychological harm from gambling marketing. The scale and range of these breaches point to serious weaknesses in TAB’s compliance systems. Continue reading...
body.zh: 关注今天的新闻直播 获取我们的突发新闻电子邮件、免费应用程序或每日新闻播客 新南威尔士州州长克里斯·明斯 (Chris Minns) 表示，该州工党受到“一国党”的威胁，“我们必须为这些选票而战”。今天早上，明斯在美国广播公司国家广播电台上被问及一国党是否是一个“种族主义政党”。他说，对移民水平感到担忧的人不一定是种族主义者，但宝琳·汉森所说的一些话是种族主义者。当然，宝琳·汉森所说的一些话绝对是种族主义的，当她说没有好穆斯林这样的东西时。我的意思是，你还能如何描述这一点？你必须与人坦诚相待。显然是这样。但我不认为目前支持他们的每个人都是种族主义者……我们必须为这些选票而战。我认为，由于一国党的选票激增，我们的一大堆席位都受到威胁……因此，我们正在对新南威尔士州的政策进行一些我们认为是实际常识性的改变。当人们加入谢绝来电登记或取消订阅营销信息时，他们就做出了明确的选择。这些选择必须得到尊重——特别是考虑到赌博营销造成的经济损失和心理伤害的风险更高。这些违规行为的规模和范围暴露了 TAB 合规系统的严重缺陷。继续阅读...

reflection.en: When public figures comment on sensitive issues like domestic violence, their words carry significant weight. Yahweh's word reminds us to treat others, especially within domestic spheres, with consideration and honor. Victim-blaming undermines the dignity of those who have suffered and fails to uphold the respect due to every individual.
reflection.zh: 当公众人物评论家庭暴力等敏感问题时，他们的话语具有重要分量。雅伟的话语提醒我们，要以体谅和尊重对待他人，尤其是在家庭关系中。指责受害者不仅损害了受害者的尊严，也未能维护每个人应得的尊重。

verse.textEn: Husbands, in the same way be considerate as you live with your wives, and treat them with respect as the weaker partner and as heirs with you of the gracious gift of life.
verse.textZh: 你们作丈夫的，也要按情理和妻子同住；因她比你软弱，与你一同承受生命之恩的，所以要敬重她，这样便叫你们的祷告没有阻碍。

title.en: ‘A great thrill’: baby puffin is first one seen on Dorset coast in three decades
title.zh: “巨大的喜悦”：多塞特海岸三十年来首次发现海鹦幼鸟

summary.en: Volunteers on the Dorset coast have celebrated the first baby puffin sighting in three decades, a significant moment for efforts to save the last regular nesting site on mainland southern England. Their years of patient observation and dedication have finally been rewarded with this joyful discovery.
summary.zh: 多塞特海岸的志愿者们庆祝了三十年来首次发现海鹦幼鸟，这对于拯救英格兰南部大陆上最后一个已知海鹦筑巢点来说是一个重要的时刻。他们多年的耐心观察和奉献终于得到了回报，迎来了这一令人欣喜的发现。

body.en: Puffling excites volunteers trying to save the last known regular nesting site on mainland of southern England For years, volunteers have craned their necks and tested their patience to peer into the crevices of the cliffs hoping for a glimpse of fluffy joy. Finally it all paid off as a puffling – baby puffin – poked its way into the sunshine, becoming the first to be spotted on the coast of Dorset in southern England for three decades. Continue reading...
body.zh: 海雀让志愿者们兴奋不已，他们试图拯救英格兰南部大陆最后一个已知的常规筑巢地。多年来，志愿者们一直伸长脖子，考验自己的耐心，窥视悬崖的缝隙，希望能看到毛茸茸的快乐。最终，这一切得到了回报，一只小海雀在阳光下探出路，成为三十年来第一只在英格兰南部多塞特海岸被发现的海雀。继续阅读...

reflection.en: The long wait and patient dedication of these volunteers remind us that sustained effort, even through periods of uncertainty, can yield profound joy. Yahweh often uses our perseverance in small, faithful acts to bring about beautiful and hopeful outcomes.
reflection.zh: 这些志愿者漫长的等待和耐心的奉献提醒我们，即使在不确定的时期，持续的努力也能带来深刻的喜悦。雅伟常常使用我们在微小而忠实的行动中的坚韧，来成就美好而充满希望的成果。

verse.textEn: We also glory in our sufferings, because we know that suffering produces perseverance; perseverance, character; and character, hope.
verse.textZh: 就是在患难中也是欢欢喜喜的；因为知道患难生忍耐，忍耐生老练，老练生盼望。

title.en: France passes law banning under-15s from social media
title.zh: 法国通过法律禁止15岁以下青少年使用社交媒体

summary.en: The law will require social media platforms in France to verify all users age from January.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及公义、尊严与问责。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
France's parliament has approved a law to ban social media for under-15s from January 2027, making it the first European country to block young people from the platforms.

The law will mean everyone in France must verify their age to access social media and comes as the UK and EU are developing their own limits in response to concerns for children's mental health.

French President Emmanuel Macron has welcomed the move, which he had pledged to introduce to mark the end of his decade in office.

While sceptics have questioned the law's viability, the government has insisted the online tools to put the age checks in place are effective and safe.

Both the French Senate and National Assembly adopted the ban on Tuesday, despite criticism from some on the left.

Once the ban is in place, social media platforms would need to use age-verification tools approved by the French privacy regulator, according to Reuters news agency.

But concerns have been raised over privacy, the efficacy of age-verification tools, the risks of young people bypassing them, and how quickly the ban has been designed and brought in, Agence France Presse reports.

French Digital Minister Anne Le Hénanff defended the speed of the law's implementation ahead of the vote "because age-verification tools already exist", the agency added.

Despite Australia banning under-16s from social media in December, it is widely acknowledged that many continue to use the platforms.

In March, Australia's eSafety Commission announced seven out of 10 children aged under 16 who had a social media account before the ban still had "some access".

Given this, Professor of Internet Studies at Western Australia's Curtin University Tama Leaver told the BBC the ban has "failed" in its technical aims.

But, he says, it has successfully shown a ban "can be done" though classifies it as "a bit of an experiment".

One lesson other countries can learn from its example is that "young people need to be part of this conversation" so that a ban is "done with them" and not "to them".

Leaver said France's ban may have a higher chance of succeeding because of the requirement for every person to verify their age but that this will also increase risks to privacy.

Additional risks of the ban include pushing young people into less-regulated online spaces, reducing their news consumption and their political empowerment, he added.

Legislation restricting young people's access to social media has been ramping up around Europe in the past few months.

In June, former British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer announced under-16s in the UK will be banned from social media from January 2027. An optional midnight curfew for UK teens aged 16 and 17 was also announced.

body.zh: 法国议会批准了一项法律，从 2027 年 1 月起禁止 15 岁以下青少年使用社交媒体，使其成为第一个阻止年轻人使用这些平台的欧洲国家。 这项法律将意味着法国的每个人都必须验证自己的年龄才能访问社交媒体，与此同时，英国和欧盟正在制定自己的限制，以应对对儿童心理健康的担忧。 法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙对这一举措表示欢迎，他曾承诺将推出这一举措，以纪念他执政十年的结束。 尽管怀疑者质疑该法律的可行性，但政府坚称用于年龄检查的在线工具是有效且安全的。 尽管遭到一些左翼人士的批评，法国参议院和国民议会均于周二通过了该禁令。 据路透社报道，一旦禁令实施，社交媒体平台将需要使用法国隐私监管机构批准的年龄验证工具。 但据法新社报道，人们对隐私、年龄验证工具的有效性、年轻人绕过这些工具的风险以及禁令的设计和实施速度提出了担忧。 该机构补充说，法国数字部长 Anne Le Hénanff 在投票前为该法律的实施速度进行了辩护，“因为年龄验证工具已经存在”。 尽管澳大利亚于 12 月禁止 16 岁以下青少年使用社交媒体，但人们普遍承认许多人仍在使用这些平台。 今年 3 月，澳大利亚电子安全委员会宣布，在禁令实施前拥有社交媒体帐户的 16 岁以下儿童中，有十分之七的人仍具有“部分访问权限”。 鉴于此，西澳大利亚科廷大学互联网研究教授 Tama Leaver 告诉 BBC，该禁令在其技术目标上“失败”了。 但是，他说，尽管将其归类为“一点实验”，但它已经成功地表明了禁令“可以做到”。 其他国家可以从这个例子中学到的一个教训是，“年轻人需要成为这场对话的一部分”，以便禁令是“与他们一起完成”，而不是“针对他们”。 利弗表示，法国的禁令成功的机会可能更高，因为每个人都需要验证自己的年龄，但这也会增加隐私风险。 他补充说，该禁令的其他风险包括将年轻人推向监管较少的网络空间，减少他们的新闻消费和政治权力。 过去几个月，欧洲各地不断加强限制年轻人使用社交媒体的立法。 6 月，英国前首相基尔·斯塔默爵士宣布，从 2027 年 1 月起，英国 16 岁以下青少年将被禁止使用社交媒体。还宣布对 16 岁和 17 岁的英国青少年实行可选的午夜宵禁。 五月，委员会主席乌苏拉·冯·德·莱恩 (Ursula von der Leyen) 提出“......

reflection.en: This story draws attention to justice, dignity, and accountability. Micah 6:8 reminds us to meet sharp headlines with justice tempered by mercy, asking how faithful people can respond with wisdom instead of noise.

title.en: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
title.zh: 日本迎来首个“酷热日”，城市酷热达 40 度

summary.en: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees...
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及和平与克制。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common. The central Japan cities of Gujo and Tajimi in Gifu prefecture, along with Toyota in Aichi prefecture, all hit 40C. Gujo’s 40C and Toyota’s 40.1C were all-time highs recorded at their respective weather stations. Continue reading...
body.zh: 4月，日本创造了新术语“kokushobi”来形容高于40摄氏度的天气，建议员工穿短裤去办公室以应对闷热的天气。周二，日本迎来了自4月份引入新术语“酷热日”以来第一个正式的“酷热日”（kokushobi），即“酷热日”。日本气象厅创造了这个新名称，以警告公众注意曾经是反常天气事件但现在变得更加常见的天气的高温危险。日本中部城市岐阜县郡上市和多治见市以及爱知县丰田市的气温均达到 40 摄氏度。郡上市的 40 摄氏度和丰田市的 40.1 摄氏度是各自气象站记录的历史最高温度。继续阅读...

title.en: Michelin-starred restaurateur faces jail for using ants as dessert garnish
title.zh: 米其林星级餐厅老板因使用蚂蚁作为甜点装饰而面临牢狱之灾

summary.en: Officials say the restaurant in South Korea used some 49,000 ants in various dishes over four years.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及和平与克制。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
South Korean prosecutors sought a one-year jail term for the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant accused of using ants as a dessert garnish, local media reported.

Ants are not among the 10 insect species approved for human consumption under the country's laws. The approved list includes grasshoppers, locusts and two-spotted crickets, among others.

Prosecutors on Monday also asked that the restaurant, which holds two Michelin stars, be fined 20 million Korean won ($13,510; £10,100).

The owner admitted to serving ants but argued that they were used in a "small part" of the restaurant's 15-course menu – as a topping for sorbets – and that diners were told they could opt for non-ant toppings.

The case came to light after officials from the Ministry of Food and Drug Safety found reviews that diners had posted online with photographs of dishes sprinkled with ants.

Investigators estimate that some 49,000 ants, imported from the US and Thailand, were used in several dishes over four years.

Health authorities also found that the restaurant used ants that "contained up to 55 times more heavy metals than typical edible insects", according to a report on The Chosun Daily.

South Korean authorities consider several criteria in deciding whether insects can be approved as food. These include food safety risks, toxicity, nutritional value, and whether they can be bred and processed hygienically.

In its defence the restaurant owner argued that just 60% of diners had opted to try the ants, while the rest were served alternatives like fermented vinegar and edible flowers.

He added that restaurants in other countries, such as Australia, Denmark and the UK, also use ants as ingredients.

Court documents did not name the restaurant nor its owner. Ten restaurants in South Korea, all located in capital city Seoul, have been awarded two Michelin stars.

The Michelin Guide awards up to three stars to eateries based on ingredient quality, flavour mastery, cooking technique, chef's personality and consistency.

Two stars denote restaurants that do "excellent cooking" and are "worth a detour", according to Michelin's criteria.

Ants are eaten, sometimes even valued as a delicacy, in several Asian, African and Latin American cultures. Weaver ants and their eggs are used in Thai and Cambodian cooking, while in Colombia, queen ants are as prized as caviar .
body.zh: 据当地媒体报道，韩国检察官寻求对一家米其林星级餐厅的老板判处一年监禁，该餐厅被指控使用蚂蚁作为甜点装饰。 根据该国法律，蚂蚁不属于批准供人类食用的 10 种昆虫之一。批准的名单包括蚱蜢、蝗虫和二斑蟋蟀等。 检察官周一还要求对这家拥有两颗米其林星的餐厅处以 2000 万韩元（13,510 美元；10,100 英镑）的罚款。 店主承认提供了蚂蚁，但辩称它们只用于餐厅 15 道菜菜单的“一小部分”——作为冰糕的配料——并且食客被告知他们可以选择非蚂蚁配料。 食品药品安全部官员发现，食客在网上发布的评论中附有撒满蚂蚁的菜肴照片，此案曝光。 调查人员估计，四年来，从美国和泰国进口的约 49,000 只蚂蚁被用于制作多种菜肴。 据《朝鲜日报》报道，卫生当局还发现，该餐厅使用的蚂蚁“重金属含量比典型食用昆虫高出 55 倍”。 韩国当局在决定昆虫是否可以被批准作为食品时考虑了几个标准。其中包括食品安全风险、毒性、营养价值以及是否可以卫生地饲养和加工。 餐馆老板在辩护中辩称，只有 60% 的食客选择尝试蚂蚁，而其余的食客则选择了发酵醋和食用花等替代品。 他补充说，澳大利亚、丹麦和英国等其他国家的餐馆也使用蚂蚁作为食材。 法庭文件没有提及该餐厅及其所有者的名字。韩国有 10 家餐厅均位于首都首尔，荣获米其林两星。 《米其林指南》根据食材质量、风味掌握、烹饪技术、厨师个性和一致性，向餐馆授予最多三颗星。 根据米其林的标准，两颗星表示餐厅“烹饪出色”并且“值得绕道而行”。 在亚洲、非洲和拉丁美洲的一些文化中，蚂蚁被食用，有时甚至被视为美味佳肴。织蚁和它们的卵用于泰国和柬埔寨的烹饪，而在哥伦比亚，蚁后和鱼子酱一样珍贵。

title.en: Trump ally wins Republican primary for Arizona governor, setting up race against Katie Hobbs
title.zh: 特朗普盟友赢得亚利桑那州州长共和党初选，与凯蒂·霍布斯展开竞争

summary.en: The victory for Andy Biggs, who was a central figure in the effort to overturn Trump’s 2020 election defeat, sets up a high-stakes contest in the battleground state Arizona voters on Tuesday chose their nominees for governor, Congress and other top offices, setting up a fall...
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及领导、智慧与公共责任。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en: The victory for Andy Biggs, who was a central figure in the effort to overturn Trump’s 2020 election defeat, sets up a high-stakes contest in the battleground state Arizona voters on Tuesday chose their nominees for governor, Congress and other top offices, setting up a fall campaign that will test whether Republicans can reverse some of the Trump-era losses they suffered in a premier battleground state. Andy Biggs, the hard-right congressman endorsed by Donald Trump, easily won the Republican nomination to challenge the incumbent Democrat, governor Katie Hobbs. Continue reading...
body.zh: 安迪·比格斯是扭转特朗普2020年大选失利努力的核心人物，他的胜利在战场州亚利桑那州掀起了一场高风险的竞争。周二，亚利桑那州选民选出了州长、国会和其他高层职位的提名人，开启了一场秋季竞选活动，考验共和党是否能扭转特朗普时代在这个重要战场州所遭受的一些损失。得到唐纳德·特朗普支持的极右翼国会议员安迪·比格斯轻松赢得共和党提名，挑战现任民主党州长凯蒂·霍布斯。继续阅读...

reflection.en: This story draws attention to leadership, wisdom, and public responsibility. Proverbs 11:14 reminds us to meet sharp headlines with wisdom, integrity, and service, asking how faithful people can respond with wisdom instead of noise.

Yahweh's wisdom calls us to consider how we might respond to perceived wrongs not by mirroring evil, but by seeking ways to overcome it with good. This approach, though challenging, offers a path toward breaking cycles of conflict...
reflection.zh: 在威胁与反威胁不断升级的局势中，似乎除了以牙还牙的循环之外别无选择。雅伟的智慧呼召我们思考，如何回应被视为的错误，不是以恶报恶，而是寻求以善胜恶的方式。这种方法虽然充满挑战，但提供了一条打破冲突循环而非使其持续下去的道路。

title.en: US renews strikes on Iran as Trump threatens to attack underground nuclear site
title.zh: 特朗普威胁攻击地下核设施，美国再次对伊朗发动打击

summary.en: The US president's threat to target a key Iranian nuclear site came ahead of the 11th consecutive night of attacks.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及和平与克制。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
The US launched strikes on Iran for the 11th consecutive night late on Tuesday, as Tehran warned the US against targeting its nuclear facilities.

US Central Command (Centcom) said the wave of strikes were "designed to continue degrading Iran's ability to threaten commercial shipping" in the Strait of Hormuz.

Iranian state media reported that air defences were activated in the capital Tehran. Explosions were also reported in Tabriz, Chabahar, Konarak, and in Bushehr, home to Iran's only nuclear power plant.

Iran, which has retaliated against the US and its Middle East allies, warned of reprisals after Donald Trump said the US would soon hit an underground nuclear site near Natanz.

Iran and the US agreed a ceasefire in April, but hostilities resumed last week as both countries battled over control of the vital Strait of Hormuz waterway.

Centcom said US forces had targeted Iranian military operations centres, maritime capabilities, aircraft hangars, drone storage facilities, and military logistics infrastructure.

It also accused Iran of attacking "more than 30 commercial vessels" passing through the Strait of Hormuz over the past three months.

"The unwarranted attacks have endangered hundreds of innocent mariners and undermined freedom of navigation," it said, adding the waterway remained "open for commercial vessel transit".

Kuwait's army said early on Wednesday morning that its air defences were engaging with drone attacks from Iran, while Bahrain and Jordan said they had also intercepted drone and missile strikes.

Earlier the US president said the next military target could be a complex known as Pickaxe Mountain , an underground nuclear site where Iran is suspected of building an undeclared enrichment facility.

"We'll be hitting that area pretty soon, and very heavily," Trump said, addressing reporters during a White House meeting with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

He added that he would not normally announce targets but "there's not a thing they can do about it".

Iran's Khatam Al-Anbiya military command said it would consider such an attack "as an expansion of war in the region", threatening strikes on "all the interests of America, the allies and supporters", state-run broadcaster IRIB reported.

Trump also threatened to "take care of" the Houthi rebels if the Iran-backed militant group in Yemen followed through on its threatened blockade of Saudi ports.

A Houthi media official had earlier said they were closing the Bab al-Mandab Strait - the Red Sea's southern gateway - to Saudi vessels.

The Red Sea has become crucial for oil shipments from Saudi Arabia during the US-Israeli war with Iran, which has led to the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

Following the Houthi announcement, two tankers which had just loaded Saudi...
body.zh: 周二晚间，美国连续第 11 次对伊朗发动袭击，德黑兰警告美国不要针对其核设施。 美国中央司令部（Centcom）表示，这波袭击旨在“继续削弱伊朗威胁霍尔木兹海峡商业航运的能力”。 伊朗官方媒体报道称，首都德黑兰启动了防空系统。据报道，大不里士、恰巴哈尔、科纳拉克和伊朗唯一核电站所在地布什尔也发生了爆炸。 在唐纳德·特朗普表示美国将很快袭击纳坦兹附近的地下核设施后，对美国及其中东盟友进行报复的伊朗警告称，将采取报复行动。 伊朗和美国于四月份同意停火，但上周两国为争夺重要的霍尔木兹海峡水道的控制权而展开敌对行动。 中央司令部表示，美军的目标是伊朗军事行动中心、海上能力、飞机库、无人机储存设施和军事后勤基础设施。 它还指责伊朗在过去三个月里袭击了通过霍尔木兹海峡的“30多艘商船”。 声明称：“无端袭击危及了数百名无辜海员，并破坏了航行自由。”并补充说，该水道仍然“对商船通行开放”。 科威特军队周三凌晨表示，其防空系统正在应对来自伊朗的无人机袭击，而巴林和约旦表示，他们也拦截了无人机和导弹袭击。 美国总统早些时候表示，下一个军事目标可能是一个名为镐山的综合体，这是一个地下核设施，伊朗怀疑在那里建造了一个未申报的浓缩设施。 特朗普在白宫与黎巴嫩总统约瑟夫·奥恩会面时对记者说：“我们很快就会对这个地区进行非常严厉的打击。” 他补充说，他通常不会宣布目标，但“他们对此无能为力”。 据国营广播公司 IRIB 报道，伊朗哈塔姆·安比亚军事指挥部表示，将把此类袭击视为“该地区战争的扩大”，并威胁对“美国、盟友和支持者的所有利益”进行打击。 特朗普还威胁说，如果伊朗支持的也门激进组织胡塞武装兑现其对沙特港口的封锁威胁，他将“照顾”胡塞叛军。 胡塞武装媒体官员早些时候表示，他们正在对沙特船只关闭红海南部门户曼德海峡。 在美以与伊朗的战争期间，红海对于沙特阿拉伯的石油运输至关重要，导致霍尔木兹海峡实际上被关闭。 胡塞武装宣布消息后，两艘刚刚装载沙特的油轮...

title.en: Gruesome images in D4vd hearing are 'worst nightmare' for teen girl's family
title.zh: D4vd 听证会上的可怕图像对少女的家人来说是“最糟糕的噩梦”

summary.en: The court hears the singer ordered a body bag, chainsaws and an inflatable pool to dispose of the teen's body.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及公义、尊严与问责。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
Gruesome images of a teenage girl's dismembered corpse have been shown in court before her horrified parents on the opening day of a preliminary hearing in the murder case against the singer who performs under the stage name D4vd.

Prosecutors presented graphic crime scene photos showing 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez's body when it was found last year inside a Tesla registered to singer David Anthony Burke in Hollywood, Los Angeles.

A homicide detective testified that Burke, 21, had ordered a body bag, chainsaws, an inflatable pool and a shovel online in the days after Celeste's death.

Burke has pleaded not guilty to charges of murdering and sexually abusing the girl.

The grisly details had previously only been described in court filings. Tuesday's hearing marked the first time that the evidence and photographs were shown publicly in court.

Although this is not the trial itself, prosecutors are laying out the evidence they say links Burke to Celeste's death as they seek to convince the judge there is sufficient evidence for the case to proceed to trial.

Prosecutors say Burke stabbed Celeste before dismembering her body with a chainsaw to conceal the killing after years of alleged sexual abuse.

They allege he killed her because she had threatened to reveal his relationship with a minor and destroy his multi-million dollar music career.

For Celeste's parents, it was an ordeal they had prepared themselves for but could never fully anticipate, according to their lawyer.

"This is the first time they've heard this evidence in detail, and they're horrified," the family's attorney, Patrick Steinfeld, told the BBC after the court hearing.

"There was an image of Celeste's torso and her head in the trunk of the car, and the prosecutor had to ask what it was because you could not tell it was a head. It was absolutely deplorable.

Steinfeld said Celeste's mother, Mercedes, had been warned in advance that graphic images would be shown.

"I told her she didn't have to be in the courtroom for this part of the hearing," he said.

"But she said, 'I want to be here to show support for Celeste.' She just put her head down and started crying."

He said he had advised both parents that once they saw the images, they would never be able to "unsee" them.

"I encouraged them to step out for a short period of time, and they decided no - they wanted to be here to show they're supporting their daughter."

"They're an extremely private family," he added. "It's a parent's worst nightmare."

The small downtown Los Angeles courtroom was packed long before Tuesday's hearing began.

Journalists, YouTubers, TikTok creators and members of the public had entered a lottery for one of the limited number of seats to watch the proceedings.

His handcuffs were removed, but he...
body.zh: 在针对艺名 D4vd 的歌手谋杀案初步听证会的开幕当天，法庭上向她惊恐的父母展示了一名少女被肢解的可怕照片。 检察官出示了犯罪现场照片，显示 14 岁的塞莱斯特·里瓦斯·埃尔南德斯 (Celeste Rivas Hernandez) 的尸体，去年在洛杉矶好莱坞一辆注册为歌手大卫·安东尼·伯克 (David Anthony Burke) 的特斯拉车内被发现。 一名凶杀案侦探作证称，21 岁的伯克在塞莱斯特死后几天在网上订购了裹尸袋、电锯、充气泳池和铲子。 伯克对谋杀和性虐待女孩的指控表示不认罪。 这些可怕的细节此前只在法庭文件中描述过。周二的听证会标志着证据和照片首次在法庭上公开展示。 虽然这不是审判本身，但检察官正在列出他们所说的将伯克与塞莱斯特的死亡联系起来的证据，因为他们试图说服法官有足够的证据让案件进入审判阶段。 检察官称，伯克刺伤了塞莱斯特，然后用电锯肢解了她的尸体，以掩盖多年来涉嫌性虐待的杀戮。 他们声称他杀了她，因为她威胁要揭露他与一名未成年人的关系，并毁掉他价值数百万美元的音乐事业。 据塞莱斯特的律师称，对于塞莱斯特的父母来说，这是一场他们早已做好准备但无法完全预料到的磨难。 “这是他们第一次详细听到这些证据，他们感到震惊，”该家庭的律师帕特里克斯坦菲尔德在法庭听证会后告诉英国广播公司。 “汽车后备箱里有一张塞莱斯特躯干和头部的图像，检察官不得不询问那是什么，因为你无法分辨出这是一个头部。这绝对是令人遗憾的。” 斯坦菲尔德说，塞莱斯特的母亲梅塞德斯已提前收到警告，将显示图形图像。 “我告诉她，这部分听证会她不必出庭，”他说。 “但她说，‘我想来这里表达对塞莱斯特的支持。’她只是低下头开始哭泣。” 他说，他已经告诉父母，一旦他们看到这些图像，他们就永远无法“看不见”它们。 “我鼓励他们出去短暂一段时间，但他们决定不——他们想来这里表明他们对女儿的支持。” “他们是一个非常私密的家庭，”他补充道。 “这是父母最可怕的噩梦。” 早在周二的听证会开始之前，洛杉矶市中心的小型法庭就挤满了人。 记者、YouTube 用户、TikTok 创作者和公众参与了抽签活动，获得了有限的观看整个过程的座位。 他的手铐被解开了，但他...

title.en: Iran war has cost US $37.5bn so far, Hegseth says, as Pentagon seeks billions more
title.zh: 赫格斯表示，伊朗战争迄今已造成 375 亿美元损失，五角大楼正在寻求数十亿美元的额外援助

summary.en: The hearing was disrupted by protests and a shouting match as Hegseth testified at a pivotal time in the war.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及和平与克制。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
The war with Iran has cost the US some $37.5bn (£28bn) so far - an increase of nearly $8bn since the last estimate in May, Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth has said.

Hegseth told the Senate Appropriations Committee it was urgent that lawmakers approve another $87bn in congressional funding for the Pentagon, with $67bn of that destined for operations in the Middle East.

The hearing was disrupted several times by anti-war protesters and ended in a shouting match between the defence secretary and a senator.

It was Hegseth's first appearance on Capitol Hill since hostilities between the US and Iran restarted last week and comes after three American soldiers were confirmed killed in the region.

The White House asked Congress in April for $1.5tn for the Pentagon over the next fiscal year, which would raise American military spending to an all-time high in the modern era.

The US carried out an 11th consecutive day of strikes on Tuesday. Iran has been retaliating with strikes of its own against the US and its allies in the Middle East.

The defence secretary began Tuesday's testimony by saying, "we live in a dangerous world" and that it required bold and swift action.

After being interrupted by protesters, Hegseth went on to say his funding request came at an urgent time.

"Without these funds, we face critical shortfalls that threaten our ability to also simply pay our service members, rapidly replenish equipment and munitions, and sustain vital operations without disruption," Hegseth said.

Every dollar appropriated, he added, would be used to build the "lethality" of US military forces and "reinforce peace through strength".

Senate Appropriations Committee chairwoman Susan Collins, a Republican, questioned Hegseth over what the impact would be if the budget needs were not met.

Hegseth said current and future training for military members would need to be cut without the funds, as he repeatedly blamed former President Joe Biden's administration for what he said was an underfunded military.

The most senior Democrat on the committee, Patty Murray, pushed back on the funding request, saying it "does not make a lot of sense".

Asked by Democratic Senator Dick Durbin if he had an estimate for how much the war in Iran has cost so far, Hegseth gave the $37.5bn figure.

Two Democratic sources and a third source familiar with the matter told the BBC's media partner CBS that they believe the actual cost of the Iran war is much higher.

They said the current estimate did not include costs like construction for damaged American bases.

There had been no official government estimate of the US financial cost of the war since 12 May, when a senior Pentagon official put the figure at $29bn.

body.zh: 美国国防部长皮特·赫格斯表示，迄今为止，与伊朗的战争已使美国损失约 375 亿美元（280 亿英镑），比 5 月份的上次估计增加了近 80 亿美元。 赫格斯告诉参议院拨款委员会，议员们迫切需要批准为五角大楼另外提供 870 亿美元的国会资金，其中 670 亿美元用于中东行动。 The hearing was disrupted several times by anti-war protesters and ended in a shouting match between the defence secretary and a senator. 这是自上周美国和伊朗之间的敌对行动重启以来赫格赛斯首次出现在国会山，此前三名美国士兵被证实在该地区丧生。 白宫于 4 月份要求国会在下一财年为五角大楼提供 1.5 兆美元，这将使美国的军费开支达到现代历史最高水平。 The US carried out an 11th consecutive day of strikes on Tuesday.伊朗一直在对美国及其中东盟友发动报复性打击。 国防部长在周二的证词开始时表示，“我们生活在一个危险的世界”，需要采取大胆而迅速的行动。 After being interrupted by protesters, Hegseth went on to say his funding request came at an urgent time. 赫格斯说：“如果没有这些资金，我们将面临严重的短缺，威胁到我们支付军人工资、快速补充设备和弹药以及在不中断的情况下维持重要行动的能力。” 他补充说，拨款的每一美元都将用于增强美国军队的“杀伤力”并“通过实力加强和平”。 参议院拨款委员会主席、共和党人苏珊·柯林斯向赫格斯询问，如果预算需求得不到满足，将会产生什么影响。 赫格斯表示，如果没有资金，当前和未来的军事人员培训就需要削减，他一再指责前总统乔·拜登政府造成了他所说的军队资金不足。 The most senior Democrat on the committee, Patty Murray, pushed back on the funding request, saying it "does not make a lot of sense". 当民主党参议员迪克·德宾 (Dick Durbin) 问他是否估计伊朗战争迄今为止造成的损失时，赫格斯给出了 375 亿美元的数字。 两名民主党消息人士和第三名熟悉此事的消息人士告诉英国广播公司的媒体合作伙伴哥伦比亚广播公司，他们认为伊朗战争的实际成本要高得多。 他们表示，目前的估计不包括受损美国基地的建设成本。 自 5 月 12 日五角大楼高级官员称这一数字为 290 亿美元以来，政府一直没有对这场战争的美国财政成本进行官方估计。 The Pentagon previously told lawmakers the war had cost the US some...

title.en: 'Strong concerns': Australia meets with China following ballistic missile test
title.zh: “强烈关切”：弹道导弹试验后澳大利亚与中国会面

summary.en: Penny Wong reiterated the importance of transparency to her Chinese counterpart.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及和平与克制。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
Australia has raised its concerns with China over its nuclear-capable missile test in the South Pacific, noting many of the region's nations also shared the same view.

Foreign Minister Penny Wong met with her Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi, on the sidelines of ASEAN talks in the Philippines capital, Manila, on Tuesday evening.

"You speak about the importance of mutual understanding. I agree, and I expect we will discuss our concerns ... including around peace and stability in our region, safe and professional military conduct, and the importance of transparency and reassurance of intent," Wong said in opening remarks.

Wong noted the "strong concerns" expressed by many Pacific Island countries about the submarine-launched missile earlier in July.

The two foreign ministers also discussed the importance of peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific.

Your trusted source for staying up-to-date with the world around you. Get free daily news updates and analysis, straight to your inbox.

"You've often heard me say that we will cooperate where we can and disagree where we must, and we want to continue to engage because we believe that is in our national interest," Wong said.

Australia was only given a few hours' notice by China of the test, which was launched within hours of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese signing a new defence alliance with Fiji.

Albanese slammed the launch as a "provocative act" while other Pacific nations including New Zealand, Tuvalu and Fiji condemned the incident.

Solomon Islands Prime Minister Matthew Wale was standing beside his Australian counterpart in his country's capital Honiara in early July when he said the test was "not the act of a friend".

Albanese has previously flagged a strong statement would be issued by the Pacific Islands Forum, but the group's disagreement on the wording of the text has resulted in a delay.

A foreign ministers' meeting of the forum will be held in August, where the issue is likely to be discussed.

title.en: 'Business-friendly socialism': What are new UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham's policies?
title.zh: “有利于商业的社会主义”：英国新首相安迪·伯纳姆的政策是什么？

summary.en: From energy cuts to rent controls to Brexit, here's what the United Kingdom's new prime minister wants to do.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及领导、智慧与公共责任。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
Andy Burnham, sworn in this week as the United Kingdom's seventh prime minister in a decade, has opened his tenure with a slew of new policies, branding his own political ideology as "business-friendly socialism".

Burnham was sworn in as the UK's new prime minister on Monday, replacing Keir Starmer, who resigned following months of pressure from his own MPs after just over two years in the job.

The former Greater Manchester mayor, nicknamed the 'King of the North' for his defence of the region during his nine-year stint as mayor, used his first days in office to signal a new leftward shift for the Labour government.

On his first full day, Burnham told the inaugural meeting of his cabinet that he would lead a "cost of living government".

Your trusted source for staying up-to-date with the world around you. Get free daily news updates and analysis, straight to your inbox.

Burnham has branded his political ideology "business-friendly socialism" — with a distinct focus on cost of living measures.

He has unveiled some early policies designed "to do some things that people will feel and feel quite quickly", especially those on the lowest incomes — adding that a longer-term plan would be set out later in the year.

On Tuesday, Burnham announced he will cut taxes on electricity bills — one of the main drivers of the UK's cost of living crisis — in his first move to ease pressures that have angered the public and helped stoke political instability.

Burnham said his government would remove the value-added tax from domestic electricity bills from 1 October, cutting about £45 ($86) from the average annual bill of about £1,860 ($3,554) that households pay.

"We're taking immediate action to cut taxes on energy bills, put more money in people's pockets and bring back hope," Burnham said in a statement, having pledged to give people more "breathing space" in their lives.

The government said it would be funded by cancelling Starmer's plan for a digital ID scheme that would have cost the taxpayer £1.8 billion ($3.44 billion) — but former minister Darren Jones said the ID program had been "unfunded".

The energy announcement is designed to show that Burnham will move quickly to improve living standards after years of stagnation.

He said on Monday his early policies might not "take all of the pressure off" people struggling with rising prices, and that his 10-year plan will be unveiled later this year.

During his first speech as prime minister on Monday, Burnham also said his first ambition was to end rough sleeping in the UK.

He is reportedly considering rent control measures, alongside plans to build more public housing to help bring down welfare spending.

body.zh: 安迪·伯纳姆本周宣誓就任英国十年来第七位首相，他以一系列新政策开启了自己的任期，将自己的政治意识形态称为“商业友好型社会主义”。 伯纳姆于周一宣誓就任英国新首相，接替基尔·斯塔默（Keir Starmer）。后者在上任仅两年多后，就在自己国会议员数月的压力下辞职。 这位前大曼彻斯特市长因在担任市长九年期间捍卫该地区而被昵称为“北方之王”，他在上任的第一天就向工党政府发出了新的左倾信号。 在他上任的第一天，伯纳姆在内阁首次会议上表示，他将领导一个“生活成本政府”。 您值得信赖的来源，让您了解周围世界的最新动态。直接将免费的每日新闻更新和分析发送到您的收件箱。 伯纳姆将他的政治意识形态称为“商业友好型社会主义”——特别关注生活成本措施。 他公布了一些早期政策，旨在“做一些人们会很快感受到的事情”，特别是那些收入最低的人，并补充说，将在今年晚些时候制定一项长期计划。 伯纳姆周二宣布，他将削减电费税——电费是英国生活成本危机的主要驱动因素之一——这是他为缓解激怒公众并加剧政治不稳定压力而采取的第一步举措。 伯纳姆表示，他的政府将从 10 月 1 日起取消家庭电费中的增值税，从而将家庭每年支付的平均电费约 1,860 英镑（3,554 美元）削减约 45 英镑（86 美元）。 伯纳姆在一份声明中表示：“我们正在立即采取行动，削减能源费用税，让更多的钱进入人们的口袋，带回希望。”他承诺为人们的生活提供更多的“喘息空间”。 政府表示，将通过取消斯塔默的数字身份识别计划来为其提供资金，该计划将花费纳税人 18 亿英镑（34.4 亿美元），但前部长达伦·琼斯表示，该身份识别计划“没有资金支持”。 能源公告旨在表明伯纳姆在经历多年的停滞后将迅速采取行动提高生活水平。 他周一表示，他的早期政策可能无法“减轻”那些与物价上涨作斗争的人们的所有压力，他的十年计划将于今年晚些时候公布。 伯纳姆在周一作为首相的首次演讲中还表示，他的首要目标是结束英国的露宿生活。 据报道，他正在考虑采取租金管制措施，同时计划建造更多公共住房以帮助减少福利支出。 伯纳姆承诺重新工业化......

title.en: Flights to return after 40-year hiatus as Trump promises to help Lebanon 'a lot'
title.zh: 特朗普承诺“大力”帮助黎巴嫩 航班时隔 40 年回归

summary.en: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun says his goal is for "a complete Israeli withdrawal from Lebanese territory".
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及领导、智慧与公共责任。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
United States President Donald Trump vowed on Wednesday to help Lebanon "a lot" as its president, Joseph Aoun, sought the US' backing for a fragile effort to disarm Hezbollah and forge a path to peace with Israel.

Meeting Aoun at the White House, Trump said Lebanon had been "a very mistreated country" that had been "hit hard" for decades.

"It's probably a dangerous place in a lot of ways," Trump told reporters. "But they love the country ... and we're going to help it. We're going to help it a lot."

Aoun, the first Lebanese president invited to Washington since 2009, asked Trump for political support and continued backing for the Lebanese army, which has begun deploying in southern areas under a US-backed framework agreement with Israel.

"We need to support the LAF," Aoun said, referring to the Lebanese Armed Forces. "Without the LAF, everything will collapse."

Your trusted source for staying up-to-date with the world around you. Get free daily news updates and analysis, straight to your inbox.

Aoun's nearly two-hour visit took place as Israeli troops continue to occupy a swath of the country's south and hundreds of thousands of Lebanese remain displaced following Israeli strikes. Hezbollah has rejected the government's direct talks with Israel as well as efforts by the state to disarm it.

The talks highlighted Aoun's delicate balancing act — seeking Trump's help to secure an Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon while simultaneously facing pressure from the US and Israel to disarm Hezbollah, which remains a formidable fighting force despite recent setbacks.

The Lebanese president, a former army commander, said the military was "the backbone of security and stability" and the country's most trusted institution.

In a statement after the talks, the Lebanese presidency said Aoun stressed to Trump the "urgent need for a complete Israeli withdrawal from Lebanese territory," calling it "an essential step to consolidate stability and enable the Lebanese state to extend its full sovereignty exclusively through its own forces."

Following his meeting with Aoun, Trump said he would direct his administration to allow all US airline carriers to fly directly to Lebanon— a historic policy shift more than 40 years in the making.

US flights were suspended in 1985 after the hijacking of TWA Flight 847 from Athens, Greece, en route to Rome, Italy, that was forced to land in Beirut. During the 17-day ordeal, a US Navy diver was brutally killed.

The Federal Aviation Administration and Transportation Department did not immediately comment.

Trump's visit came as the Lebanese army moved into an area of the south under a deal with Israel aimed at proving Lebanon can extend state authority and disarm Iran-backed Hezbollah.

title.en: 'It's really going to hurt': The oil route helping keep prices down is now under threat
title.zh: “这真的会造成伤害”：有助于压低油价的石油路线现在正受到威胁

summary.en: If Yemen's Houthi rebels successfully blockade Saudi Arabian ships, global oil supplies could come under more pressure.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及和平与克制。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
There are concerns global energy supplies could be crippled and oil prices spike even further after Yemen's Houthi rebels opened up a new front in the US-Israeli war on Iran by vowing to impose a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia.

And it could put added pressure on the Albanese government to extend the fuel excise discount — which is due to end on 2 August.

Energy supplies have already been stifled by volatility in the Strait of Hormuz — now at the centre of a continuing war that was originally intended by the US and Israel in February to target any Iranian nuclear weapon capability.

Though it's not clear how the Houthis would carry out their campaign against Saudi Arabia, it threatens to disrupt shipping in the Red Sea , through which the Saudis have been exporting millions of barrels of oil.

Lurion de Mello, a senior lecturer in applied finance at Macquarie University, said oil prices, especially diesel, could take a hit.

Your trusted source for staying up-to-date with the world around you. Get free daily news updates and analysis, straight to your inbox.

"Saudi Arabia has been pumping quite a fair bit of oil, and exporting it through its ports in the Red Sea, and this oil has been making its way all the way to China and also into refineries into Asia," he told SBS News.

"So this threat, I think it's really going to hurt the supply coming out of Saudi Arabia, and I think definitely will have an uptick on the oil price."

The Yemeni militant group has been fighting guerrilla wars against the Saudi-backed Yemeni government for years. It is aligned with Iran, which considers the Houthis as part of its regional "Axis of Resistance", though the Houthis deny being an Iranian proxy and say they develop their own weapons.

It is not clear how far the group's new blockade campaign stems from its own strategic priorities or is being made on Iran's behalf.

A long-running conflict between the Houthis and the Saudis flared up again last week, when Yemen's internationally recognised government said it had struck Sanaa airport to stop an Iranian plane landing.

The Houthis said Saudi Arabia was responsible and in response fired missiles at Abha airport in the kingdom's mountainous southwest.

Yemen sits on the Bab al-Mandab Strait — the southern gateway to the Red Sea — and closing that would open up a new front in the energy crisis and Iran's overarching conflict with the US.

The strait has emerged as a much-needed alternative to the Strait of Hormuz for global oil flows, with more than seven million barrels per day transiting the strait in June, up from roughly four million barrels before the Iran war.

Following Iran's partial blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, Saudi Arabia responded by diverting more than 70 per cent of...

title.en: Jordan faces growing risk of becoming latest frontline state in US-Iran conflict
title.zh: 约旦面临成为美伊冲突最新前线国家的日益增长的风险

summary.en: Jordan is increasingly becoming a direct participant in the escalating Middle East conflict, facing intensified attacks from Iranian forces. The Hashemite Kingdom is now seen as a new frontline in the regional tensions.
summary.zh: 随着伊朗袭击的加剧，约旦正日益成为不断扩大的中东冲突中的最新前线国家。这个哈希姆王国现在被视为地区紧张局势中的一个新战场。

body.en:
As Iranian attacks intensify, Jordan is emerging as the latest frontline state in the widening Middle East conflict. Why is the Hashemite Kingdom a target for Tehran?

Air raid sirens again sounded in parts of Jordan  early Tuesday as Iranian missiles targeted the kingdom.

"The air defense system intercepted and downed tonight three missiles coming from Iranian territory that entered Jordanian airspace and targeted the kingdom's territory," the army said in a statement.

No injuries were reported, but the latest strikes follow a series of attacks that have raised the stakes for one of the  United States' closest Arab allies in the past days: Two US service members were killed and around 30 others injured by an Iranian missile strike on a US base in Jordan last week.

Jordan is one of the eight Arab countries that host permanent US military facilities. Some of the other host countries — particularly the Gulf states — have been the  targets of Iranian attacks since the United States and Israel   launched joint attacks on Iran in late February.

Though  Iran  launched missiles toward Jordan shortly thereafter, there has since been a lull in direct attacks on the kingdom, which has helped to intercept Iranian missiles launched toward Israel, arguing that it is acting in defense of its own airspace. 

" Iran  has reserved itself the right to return fire on any state in the region that hosts US military assets or bases, and Jordan is one of them," Erwin van Veen, a senior research fellow at the Clingendael Institute in The Hague, told DW. "Only the options of Jordan's King Abdullah II are quite limited as Jordan is in a very precarious balance with respect to military and economic dependence on the Americans."

On Sunday, in a first diplomatic escalation, Jordan summoned Iran's top diplomat in Amman to demand an immediate end to what the foreign ministry called "unjustified and blatant Iranian attacks."

The US is Jordan's largest single foreign supporter. Under a seven-year Memorandum of Understanding signed in 2022, Washington agreed to provide $1.45 billion (€1.27bn) annually in economic and military assistance. In 2025, Congress increased this to $1.65 billion, which was triple the amount from 2010.

Furthermore, a Defense Cooperation Agreement signed in January 2021 grants US forces formalized access to Jordanian military facilities. According to a recent report , there are currently 4,000 US troops, more than in previous years, stationed in Jordan.

In March, however, Jordanian Foreign Minister Ayman Safadi insisted that there were no foreign military bases in the country. Kelly Petillo, program manager for Middle East and North Africa at the European Council on...
body.zh: 随着伊朗袭击加剧，约旦正在成为不断扩大的中东冲突的最新前线国家。为什么哈希姆王国成为德黑兰的目标？ 周二早些时候，伊朗导弹瞄准约旦，约旦部分地区再次拉响空袭警报。 军方在一份声明中表示：“防空系统今晚拦截并击落了三枚来自伊朗领土的导弹，这些导弹进入约旦领空并瞄准该王国领土。” 没有人员伤亡的报道，但最近的袭击是在过去几天发生的一系列袭击事件之后发生的，这些袭击增加了美国最亲密的阿拉伯盟友之一的风险：上周伊朗导弹袭击了美国驻约旦基地，造成两名美国军人死亡，约 30 人受伤。 约旦是拥有美国永久军事设施的八个阿拉伯国家之一。自二月底美国和以色列对伊朗发动联合袭击以来，其他一些东道国，尤其是海湾国家，一直是伊朗袭击的目标。 尽管伊朗此后不久就向约旦发射了导弹，但此后对约旦的直接攻击已经暂停，这有助于拦截伊朗向以色列发射的导弹，认为这是为了保卫自己的领空。 海牙克林根达尔研究所高级研究员欧文·范维恩对德国之声表示：“伊朗保留向该地区任何拥有美国军事资产或基地的国家进行还击的权利，约旦就是其中之一。” “只有约旦国王阿卜杜拉二世的选择相当有限，因为约旦在军事和经济上对美国的依赖处于非常不稳定的平衡。” 周日，在首次外交升级中，约旦召见伊朗驻安曼高级外交官，要求立即停止外交部所说的“伊朗不公正和公然的袭击”。 美国是约旦最大的单一外国支持者。根据 2022 年签署的为期七年的谅解备忘录，华盛顿同意每年提供 14.5 亿美元（12.7 亿欧元）的经济和军事援助。 2025 年，国会将这一数额增加到 16.5 亿美元，是 2010 年的三倍。 此外，2021 年 1 月签署的国防合作协议允许美军正式进入约旦军事设施。据最新报道，目前驻约旦美军有4000人，比往年多。 然而，三月份，约旦外交部长艾曼·萨法迪坚称该国没有外国军事基地。凯利·佩蒂略 (Kelly Petillo)，欧洲理事会中东和北非项目经理

reflection.en: As regional conflicts expand and draw more nations into their orbit, it can feel as though peace is an ever-receding hope. Yet, Psalm 46:9 reminds us that Yahweh ultimately has the power to make wars cease across the earth. This enduring promise offers solace and a vision of a future where even the most entrenched conflicts find their end.
reflection.zh: 当地区冲突不断扩大，将更多国家卷入其中时，和平似乎成为一个遥不可及的希望。然而，诗篇46:9提醒我们，雅伟最终有能力使战争止息，直到地极。这个持久的应许为我们提供了慰藉，并描绘了一个即使是最根深蒂固的冲突也能终结的未来。

verse.textEn: He makes wars cease to the ends of the earth; he breaks the bow and shatters the spear.
verse.textZh: 他止息刀兵，直到地极；他折弓断枪。

title.en: China narrows AI gap, challenges US influence
title.zh: 中国缩小人工智能差距，挑战美国影响力

summary.en: At the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai, Beijing showcased advances in AI while promoting its vision of open-source development and global governance.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自全球栏目的新闻，重点涉及和平与克制。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
At the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai, Beijing showcased advances in AI while promoting its vision of open-source development and global governance.

As China narrows the artificial intelligence (AI) gap with the United States,  Beijing is promoting a narrative centered on "open source" and "inclusive AI" as an argument for its vision of global governance.

During his keynote speech at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) in Shanghai on Friday, Chinese President Xi Jinping expressed China's ambition to take a leading role in setting global standards in AI . He criticized what he described as the "overextension of the concept of national security."

Xi did not openly reference the United States. However, Beijing's effort to position itself as an advocate of a new AI order and challenge US dominance was unmistakable.

This year's Shanghai summit sent several important signals, says Rogier Creemers, assistant professor at Leiden University in the Netherlands specializing in China's digital technology policy. The WAIC indicates that artificial intelligence is now one of Beijing's political priorities, and that China intends to compete with the United States by supporting open-source AI models and promoting AI-driven industrialization, Creemers told DW. He also noted China hopes to win support from countries in the Global South for this effort.

China is steadily narrowing the gap with the United States in AI model development, and its efforts at AI application in industrial processes are slowly maturing. 

Chinese AI model developers have continued to keep pace on the global stage, largely by training open-weight models on limited but advanced computer hardware, Paul Triolo, senior vice president for China and tech policy lead at DGA-Albright Stonebridge Group, told DW.

Triolo believes deployment of AI across Chinese enterprises may be progressing faster than in the United States.

In addition, there is less fear of AI among the Chinese population than there is in the US, facilitating more rapid uptake of AI applications at the consumer level.

Earlier this year, Chinese state media claimed that the performance gap between Chinese and US AI models had "largely disappeared."

Several experts broadly agreed with this assessment, saying that certain benchmark tests of China's  large language models (LLMs) such as GLM-5.2 and Kimi K3 showed the gap narrowing or even fully closing.

For most applications, Triolo noted, China's leading open-weight AI models are cheaper to run and more than capable of handling most tasks.

At the same time, advanced proprietary models from leading US companies such as Anthropic, OpenAI  and Google still retain advantages in areas such as...
body.zh: 在上海举行的世界人工智能大会上，北京展示了人工智能的进步，同时宣传其开源发展和全球治理的愿景。 随着中国缩小与美国的人工智能 (AI) 差距，北京正在推动以“开源”和“包容性人工智能”为中心的叙事，作为其全球治理愿景的论据。 中国国家主席习近平周五在上海举行的世界人工智能大会（WAIC）上发表主旨演讲时表达了中国在制定人工智能全球标准方面发挥主导作用的雄心。他批评他所说的“国家安全概念的过度延伸”。 习近平没有公开提及美国。 然而，北京将自己定位为人工智能新秩序倡导者并挑战美国主导地位的努力是明确无误的。 荷兰莱顿大学专门研究中国数字技术政策的助理教授罗吉尔·克里默斯表示，今年的上海峰会发出了几个重要信号。 Creemers 告诉德国之声，WAIC 表明人工智能现在是北京的政治优先事项之一，中国打算通过支持开源人工智能模型和促进人工智能驱动的工业化来与美国竞争。他还指出，中国希望为此赢得南方国家的支持。 中国在人工智能模型开发方面与美国的差距正在稳步缩小，人工智能在工业流程中的应用也正在慢慢成熟。 DGA-Albright Stonebridge Group 中国区高级副总裁兼技术政策负责人 Paul Triolo 告诉德国之声，中国人工智能模型开发人员继续在全球舞台上保持同步，主要是在有限但先进的计算机硬件上训练开放权重模型。 Triolo 认为，中国企业的人工智能部署进展可能比美国更快。 此外，中国民众对人工智能的恐惧程度低于美国民众，这有助于消费者更快速地接受人工智能应用。 今年早些时候，中国官方媒体声称中国和美国人工智能模型之间的性能差距已经“基本消失”。 多位专家普遍同意这一评估，并表示，GLM-5.2和Kimi K3等中国大型语言模型（LLM）的某些基准测试表明，差距正在缩小甚至完全缩小。 Triolo 指出，对于大多数应用程序来说，中国领先的开放式人工智能模型的运行成本更低，并且能够处理大多数任务。 与此同时，Anthropic、OpenAI 和 Google 等美国领先公司的先进专有模型仍然在以下领域保持优势……

reflection.en: This story draws attention to peace-making and restraint. Matthew 5:9 reminds us to meet sharp headlines with mercy, truth, and restraint, asking how faithful people can respond with wisdom instead of noise.
reflection.zh: 这则新闻让人想到和平与克制。Matthew 5:9提醒我们，在快速变化的新闻里，仍要以怜悯、真实与节制来回应，并学习用祷告与分辨代替情绪化判断。

verse.textEn: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.
verse.textZh: 使人和睦的人有福了，因为他们必称为神的儿子。

title.en: How India's youth-led 'Cockroach' movement challenges Modi
title.zh: 印度青年主导的“蟑螂”运动如何挑战莫迪

summary.en: A satirical protest in India against exam leaks has evolved into a significant youth-led movement. This movement now expresses broader discontent among young Indians regarding employment opportunities and educational issues.
summary.zh: 印度一场针对考试泄题的讽刺性抗议活动，已演变为一场由青年主导的更广泛的运动。该运动表达了印度年轻人对就业和教育问题日益增长的不满。

body.en:
What started as a satirical protest against exam leaks in India has grown into a wider movement voicing young Indians' anger over jobs and education.

Riot police fired tear gas and used batons in New Delhi as tens of thousands of supporters of the satirical Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) marched towards India's Parliament on Monday (July 20).

Police in the Indian capital said 60 protesters and 118 police officers were injured in the clashes between demonstrators and security forces. Mobile internet was suspended in parts of the city, including around the Jantar Mantar protest site, during the violence.

Protesters are demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Earlier this year, authorities canceled the high-stakes NEET medical entrance exam over a leaked question paper , affecting millions of students and sparking outrage across India .

The CJP began as an online campaign in May and rapidly evolved into a Gen Z street movement , giving a voice to a generation frustrated by unemployment, shrinking opportunities and a failing education system.

The youth-led "Cockroach" party is now posing an unexpected challenge for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government , prompting a response that combines political outreach with police action.

Modi's rivals have also tried to step in and take advantage of the youth's frustrations.

"Is this how a government behaves? Using tear gas and baton charges against students? This is a worthless government," Renuka Chowdhury, an MP from India's opposition Congress party, told DW.

The CJP movement  gained fresh momentum over the weekend after veteran reform activist Sonam Wangchuk, whose three-week hunger strike has become a symbol of the protests, was forcibly taken to a hospital by police . 

It is not the first time India has experienced major protest movements. Hunger strikes, famously used by Mahatma Gandhi, remain a common and peaceful form of protest in India.

India Against Corruption (IAC) led by veteran activist Anna Hazare, managed to mobilize millions in 2011 and reshape the country's political landscape. A decade later, yearlong farmers' protests forced Modi to repeal controversial farm laws. However, a 2023 Olympic wrestlers' protest over allegations of sexual harassment achieved few of their goals, despite widespread sympathy.

Analysts say the CJP lacks a nationwide organizational network, which the farmers had at their disposal. Yet like the IAC movement, it has tapped into a broader public mood.

"This protest is different because it reflects a much broader cross-section of Indian society," said Yamini Aiyar, an analyst and senior visiting fellow at Brown University. "They are expressing their frustration with a country whose long-atrophied education...
body.zh: 最初是针对印度考试泄密的讽刺抗议，现已发展成为一场更广泛的运动，表达印度年轻人对就业和教育的愤怒。 周一（7 月 20 日），讽刺性的蟑螂简塔党 (CJP) 的数万名支持者向印度议会游行，防暴警察发射催泪瓦斯并使用警棍。 印度首都警方表示，示威者与安全部队发生冲突，造成 60 名抗议者和 118 名警察受伤。暴力事件期间，该市部分地区的移动互联网被暂停，包括简塔·曼塔天文台抗议地点周围。 抗议者要求教育部长达门德拉·普拉丹(Dharmendra Pradhan)辞职。今年早些时候，当局因试卷泄露而取消了高风险的尼特族医学入学考试，影响了数百万学生，并引发了印度各地的愤怒。 CJP 于 5 月开始是一场在线运动，并迅速演变为 Z 世代街头运动，为因失业、机会减少和教育体系失败而沮丧的一代人发出声音。 由青年领导的“蟑螂”政党现在对纳伦德拉·莫迪总理的政府构成了意想不到的挑战，促使政府采取政治外展与警方行动相结合的应对措施。 莫迪的竞争对手也试图介入并利用年轻人的挫败感。 印度反对党国大党议员雷努卡·乔杜里 (Renuka Chowdhury) 向德国之声表示：“这就是政府的行为方式吗？对学生使用催泪瓦斯和警棍？这是一个毫无价值的政府。” 周末，资深改革活动家索南·旺楚克 (Sonam Wangchuk) 被警察强行送往医院，他的三周绝食已成为抗议活动的象征，CJP 运动获得了新的动力。 这并不是印度第一次经历大规模抗议活动。圣雄甘地著名的绝食抗议仍然是印度常见的和平抗议形式。 由资深活动人士安娜·哈扎尔 (Anna Hazare) 领导的印度反腐败组织 (IAC) 在 2011 年成功动员了数百万人，重塑了该国的政治版图。十年后，长达一年的农民抗议迫使莫迪废除有争议的农业法。然而，2023 年奥运会摔跤手针对性骚扰指控的抗议尽管得到了广泛的同情，但几乎没有实现他们的目标。 分析人士称，CJP 缺乏可供农民使用的全国性组织网络。 然而，就像 IAC 运动一样，它利用了更广泛的公众情绪。 布朗大学分析师兼高级访问学者亚米尼·艾亚尔 (Yamini Aiyar) 表示：“这次抗议活动与众不同，因为它反映了印度社会更广泛的层面。” “他们表达了对这个教育长期萎缩的国家的失望......

reflection.en: When young people feel their future is at stake due to systemic issues, their collective voice can rise as a powerful call for change. Yahweh's word reminds us that true justice should flow consistently and powerfully, addressing the deep-seated concerns of a society.
reflection.zh: 当年轻人因系统性问题感到未来受到威胁时，他们的集体声音会成为一股强大的变革呼吁。雅伟的话语提醒我们，真正的公义应如江河般持续而有力地流淌，回应社会深层关切。

verse.textEn: Let justice roll on like a river, righteousness like a never-failing stream!
verse.textZh: 惟愿公平如大水滚滚，使公义如江河滔滔。

title.en: Mexican drug lord 'El Mayo' sentenced to life without parole in US prison
title.zh: 墨西哥毒枭“埃尔·马约”在美国监狱被判无期徒刑

summary.en: Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, a co-founder of the notorious Sinaloa cartel and one of the world's most wanted drug traffickers, has been sentenced to life without parole in a US prison.
summary.zh: 臭名昭著的锡那罗亚贩毒集团联合创始人、全球头号毒枭之一伊斯梅尔·“埃尔·马约”·赞巴达，在美国被判处无期徒刑，不得假释。

body.en:
Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, a co-founder of the Sinaloa cartel and one of the world's most notorious drug traffickers, was sentenced to life in a US prison.

Mexican drug trafficker Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole on Tuesday for co-founding and leading the notorious Sinaloa cartel .

Zambada was one of the most prominent Mexican drug traffickers to face sentencing in a United States court since Joaquin "El Chapo" Guzman was sentenced to life in prison in 2019.

"The almost unimaginable amount of drugs attributable to him and the number of murders that we can document is enough to justify Congress's decision to make this a mandatory life sentence," US District Judge Brian Cogan said.

The 76-year-old Zambada apologized "for the harm I caused and for the example I set," urging future generations to "choose a different path."

He was arrested in July 2024 after he said he was kidnapped in Mexico and flown to Texas .

Zambada helped found and lead the Sinaloa cartel, becoming one of the most wanted figures for US intelligence and law enforcement agencies for decades.

According to prosecutors, he ordered torture and plotted murders, including the killing of his own nephew, as the cartel flooded the US with tons of cocaine, fentanyl, methamphetamine and other drugs .

He pleaded guilty to charges that included engaging in a continuing criminal enterprise and racketeering conspiracy, acknowledging culpability for 85 underlying crimes. In exchange, prosecutors did not seek the death penalty.

"I grew up in an environment where violence and crime seemed normal, but now I know that's no excuse," Zambada said in court on Monday. "There comes a time when every person must take responsibility for their decisions. I didn't, and many people paid the price."

Zambada's former partner "El Chapo" is already serving a life sentence in the United States.

He was convicted in 2019 and is serving a life sentence at the federal Supermax prison in Florence, Colorado.

Together they built the Sinaloa cartel into one of the world's most powerful drug trafficking organizations.

The Sinaloa cartel is one of six Mexican drug gangs that US President Donald Trump has designated as Foreign Terrorist Organizations.

Zambada was arrested with El Chapo's son, Joaquin Guzman Lopez , one of El Chapo's sons, who has pleaded guilty to federal drug charges. He has yet to be sentenced.

Zambada's arrest led to a splintering of the Sinaloa cartel, pitting his loyalists against backers of Guzman's sons, dubbed the Chapitos, or "little Chapos."

body.zh: 伊斯梅尔·“埃尔·梅奥”·赞巴达是锡那罗亚贩毒集团的联合创始人，也是世界上最臭名昭著的毒贩之一，在美国监狱被判处终身监禁。 周二，墨西哥毒贩伊斯梅尔·“埃尔·梅奥”·赞巴达因共同创立和领导臭名昭著的锡那罗亚贩毒集团而被判处终身监禁，不得假释。 自 2019 年“矮子”华金·古兹曼被判处终身监禁以来，赞巴达是在美国法院面临判刑的最著名的墨西哥毒贩之一。 美国地区法官布莱恩·科根 (Brian Cogan) 表示：“他所携带的几乎难以想象的毒品数量以及我们可以记录的谋杀案数量足以证明国会决定将其判处强制性无期徒刑。” 76岁的赞巴达“为我造成的伤害和我树立的榜样”道歉，并敦促后代“选择一条不同的道路”。 他于 2024 年 7 月被捕，此前他声称自己在墨西哥被绑架并飞往德克萨斯州。 赞巴达帮助创建并领导了锡那罗亚贩毒集团，成为美国情报和执法机构几十年来最想要的人物之一。 据检察官称，当该贩毒集团向美国大量运送可卡因、芬太尼、冰毒和其他毒品时，他下令实施酷刑并策划谋杀，包括杀害自己的侄子。 他对包括参与持续犯罪活动和共谋敲诈勒索在内的指控表示认罪，承认对 85 项基本犯罪负有责任。作为交换，检察官没有寻求死刑。 赞巴达周一在法庭上表示：“在我成长的环境中，暴力和犯罪似乎很常见，但现在我知道这不是借口。” “总有一天，每个人都必须为自己的决定负责。我没有，很多人为此付出了代价。” 赞巴达的前搭档“矮子”已经在美国服无期徒刑。 他于 2019 年被定罪，目前正在科罗拉多州佛罗伦萨的联邦 Supermax 监狱服无期徒刑。 他们共同将锡那罗亚贩毒集团打造成为世界上最强大的贩毒组织之一。 锡那罗亚贩毒集团是美国总统唐纳德·特朗普指定为外国恐怖组织的六个墨西哥贩毒团伙之一。 赞巴达与矮子的儿子华金·古兹曼·洛佩兹 (Joaquin Guzman Lopez) 一起被捕，华金·古兹曼·洛佩兹 (Joaquin Guzman Lopez) 已承认联邦毒品指控。他尚未被判刑。 赞巴达的被捕导致锡那罗亚贩毒集团的分裂，他的忠诚者与古兹曼儿子的支持者（被称为查皮托斯或“小矮子”）对立起来。 不要让算法隐藏新闻。如果您依赖我们团队提供值得信赖的报告，请花点时间选择我们作为您在 Google 上的首选来源，这样您将始终看到我们经过验证的...

title.en: 'Strong concerns': Australia meets with China following ballistic missile test
title.zh: “强烈关切”：弹道导弹试验后澳大利亚与中国会面

summary.en: Penny Wong reiterated the importance of transparency to her Chinese counterpart.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自中国栏目的新闻，重点涉及和平与克制。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
Australia has raised its concerns with China over its nuclear-capable missile test in the South Pacific, noting many of the region's nations also shared the same view.

Foreign Minister Penny Wong met with her Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi, on the sidelines of ASEAN talks in the Philippines capital, Manila, on Tuesday evening.

"You speak about the importance of mutual understanding. I agree, and I expect we will discuss our concerns ... including around peace and stability in our region, safe and professional military conduct, and the importance of transparency and reassurance of intent," Wong said in opening remarks.

Wong noted the "strong concerns" expressed by many Pacific Island countries about the submarine-launched missile earlier in July.

The two foreign ministers also discussed the importance of peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific.

Your trusted source for staying up-to-date with the world around you. Get free daily news updates and analysis, straight to your inbox.

"You've often heard me say that we will cooperate where we can and disagree where we must, and we want to continue to engage because we believe that is in our national interest," Wong said.

Australia was only given a few hours' notice by China of the test, which was launched within hours of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese signing a new defence alliance with Fiji.

Albanese slammed the launch as a "provocative act" while other Pacific nations including New Zealand, Tuvalu and Fiji condemned the incident.

Solomon Islands Prime Minister Matthew Wale was standing beside his Australian counterpart in his country's capital Honiara in early July when he said the test was "not the act of a friend".

Albanese has previously flagged a strong statement would be issued by the Pacific Islands Forum, but the group's disagreement on the wording of the text has resulted in a delay.

A foreign ministers' meeting of the forum will be held in August, where the issue is likely to be discussed.

body.zh: 澳大利亚就中国在南太平洋进行的核导弹试验向中国表达了担忧，并指出该地区许多国家也持有同样的观点。 周二晚上，外交部长黄英贤在菲律宾首都马尼拉举行的东盟会谈期间会见了中国外交部长王毅。 黄在开场白中表示：“你谈到了相互理解的重要性。我同意，并且我希望我们能够讨论我们所关心的问题……包括我们地区的和平与稳定、安全和专业的军事行为，以及透明度和保证意图的重要性。” 黄指出，许多太平洋岛国7月初对潜射导弹表达了“强烈担忧”。 两国外长还讨论了印太地区和平与稳定的重要性。 您值得信赖的来源，让您了解周围世界的最新动态。直接将免费的每日新闻更新和分析发送到您的收件箱。 黄说：“你们经常听我说，我们将在能够合作的地方合作，在必须反对的地方反对，我们希望继续参与，因为我们相信这符合我们的国家利益。” 中国仅在几个小时内向澳大利亚发出了此次试验的通知，此次试验是在澳大利亚总理安东尼·阿尔巴内塞与斐济签署新的防务联盟后数小时内启动的。 阿尔巴内斯抨击这次发射是“挑衅行为”，而新西兰、图瓦卢和斐济等其他太平洋国家则谴责这一事件。 7月初，所罗门群岛总理马修·威尔在首都霍尼亚拉站在澳大利亚总理旁边，他表示这次测试“不是朋友的行为”。 阿尔巴内塞此前曾表示太平洋岛屿论坛将发表一份强硬声明，但该组织对文本措辞的分歧导致了这一声明的推迟。 论坛外长会议将于8月举行，届时可能会讨论这一问题。 如需了解 SBS 新闻的最新动态，请下载我们的应用程序并订阅我们的时事通讯。

title.en: China narrows AI gap, challenges US influence
title.zh: 中国缩小人工智能差距，挑战美国影响力

summary.en: At the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai, Beijing showcased advances in AI while promoting its vision of open-source development and global governance.
summary.zh: 这是一则来自中国栏目的新闻，重点涉及和平与克制。如需完整细节，请阅读英文原始报道。

body.en:
At the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai, Beijing showcased advances in AI while promoting its vision of open-source development and global governance.

As China narrows the artificial intelligence (AI) gap with the United States,  Beijing is promoting a narrative centered on "open source" and "inclusive AI" as an argument for its vision of global governance.

During his keynote speech at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) in Shanghai on Friday, Chinese President Xi Jinping expressed China's ambition to take a leading role in setting global standards in AI . He criticized what he described as the "overextension of the concept of national security."

Xi did not openly reference the United States. However, Beijing's effort to position itself as an advocate of a new AI order and challenge US dominance was unmistakable.

This year's Shanghai summit sent several important signals, says Rogier Creemers, assistant professor at Leiden University in the Netherlands specializing in China's digital technology policy. The WAIC indicates that artificial intelligence is now one of Beijing's political priorities, and that China intends to compete with the United States by supporting open-source AI models and promoting AI-driven industrialization, Creemers told DW. He also noted China hopes to win support from countries in the Global South for this effort.

China is steadily narrowing the gap with the United States in AI model development, and its efforts at AI application in industrial processes are slowly maturing. 

Chinese AI model developers have continued to keep pace on the global stage, largely by training open-weight models on limited but advanced computer hardware, Paul Triolo, senior vice president for China and tech policy lead at DGA-Albright Stonebridge Group, told DW.

Triolo believes deployment of AI across Chinese enterprises may be progressing faster than in the United States.

In addition, there is less fear of AI among the Chinese population than there is in the US, facilitating more rapid uptake of AI applications at the consumer level.

Earlier this year, Chinese state media claimed that the performance gap between Chinese and US AI models had "largely disappeared."

Several experts broadly agreed with this assessment, saying that certain benchmark tests of China's  large language models (LLMs) such as GLM-5.2 and Kimi K3 showed the gap narrowing or even fully closing.

For most applications, Triolo noted, China's leading open-weight AI models are cheaper to run and more than capable of handling most tasks.

body.zh: 在上海举行的世界人工智能大会上，北京展示了人工智能的进步，同时宣传其开源发展和全球治理的愿景。 随着中国缩小与美国的人工智能 (AI) 差距，北京正在推动以“开源”和“包容性人工智能”为中心的叙事，作为其全球治理愿景的论据。 中国国家主席习近平周五在上海举行的世界人工智能大会（WAIC）上发表主旨演讲时表达了中国在制定人工智能全球标准方面发挥主导作用的雄心。他批评他所说的“国家安全概念的过度延伸”。 习近平没有公开提及美国。 然而，北京将自己定位为人工智能新秩序倡导者并挑战美国主导地位的努力是明确无误的。 荷兰莱顿大学专门研究中国数字技术政策的助理教授罗吉尔·克里默斯表示，今年的上海峰会发出了几个重要信号。 Creemers 告诉德国之声，WAIC 表明人工智能现在是北京的政治优先事项之一，中国打算通过支持开源人工智能模型和促进人工智能驱动的工业化来与美国竞争。他还指出，中国希望为此赢得南方国家的支持。 中国在人工智能模型开发方面与美国的差距正在稳步缩小，人工智能在工业流程中的应用也正在慢慢成熟。 DGA-Albright Stonebridge Group 中国区高级副总裁兼技术政策负责人 Paul Triolo 告诉德国之声，中国人工智能模型开发人员继续在全球舞台上保持同步，主要是在有限但先进的计算机硬件上训练开放权重模型。 Triolo 认为，中国企业的人工智能部署进展可能比美国更快。 此外，中国民众对人工智能的恐惧程度低于美国民众，这有助于消费者更快速地接受人工智能应用。 今年早些时候，中国官方媒体声称中国和美国人工智能模型之间的性能差距已经“基本消失”。 多位专家普遍同意这一评估，并表示，GLM-5.2和Kimi K3等中国大型语言模型（LLM）的某些基准测试表明，差距正在缩小甚至完全缩小。 Triolo 指出，对于大多数应用程序来说，中国领先的开放式人工智能模型的运行成本更低，并且能够处理大多数任务。 与此同时，Anthropic、OpenAI 和 Google 等美国领先公司的先进专有模型仍然在以下领域保持优势……

title.en: China, Philippines clash in South China Sea ahead of ASEAN meeting
title.zh: 东盟会议前夕，中国与菲律宾在南海发生冲突

summary.en: China and the Philippines traded accusations after a violent altercation between navy personnel in the disputed South China Sea. The incident heightened tensions ahead of a major diplomatic summit in Manila.
summary.zh: 在南海争议水域，中国和菲律宾海军人员发生暴力冲突后，两国相互指责。这一事件在马尼拉举行的一次重要外交峰会前夕加剧了紧张局势。

body.en:
China and the Philippines traded accusations after a violent altercation between navy personnel in the disputed South China Sea. The incident heightened tensions ahead of a major diplomatic summit in Manila.

Sailors from the Chinese and Philippine navies faced off with oars and batons in the disputed South China Sea , with Philippine officials saying that one of its sailors suffered a head injury as a result of the altercation.

The latest confrontation took place on Monday near the Second Thomas Shoal, a contested area where the two countries have repeatedly clashed before .  The  Philippines keeps a military presence in the area aboard the grounded warship BRP Sierra Madre.

Manila said that Chinese Coast Guard personnel had assaulted Filipino sailors as they approached the outpost. 

China quickly rejected this narrative. Beijing's Foreign Ministry said in a statement that the "Philippine side provoked the incident first," by approaching the Chinese vessel in small rubber boats, but then "turned around ​and made counter-accusations, distorted the facts and engaged in malicious ​hype."

On Tuesday, China summoned the Philippine ambassador in Beijing to lodge its complaints, with the Philippines quickly following suit with the Chinese counterpart in Manila.

The Second Thomas Shoal is part of the Spratly islands, a disputed archipelago in the South China Sea.

The Spratlys are key to Beijing's claim of the entire South China Sea, a key global trade route. Beijing maintains its claim to the waterway despite a 2016 ruling by the Permanent Court of Arbitration in The Hague that there is no legal basis to its argument.

China has repeatedly demanded the removal of the outpost on the BRP Sierra Madre, and surrounded the atoll with its ships in a tense territorial standoff that has dragged on for years.

The incident came just ahead of a meeting of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) which was set to begin on Tuesday with foreign ministers of the eleven member states and external partners.

Australia's Foreign Minister Penny Wong, who was in Manila for the summit, denounced Beijing on Tuesday for what she called "destabilizing and dangerous conduct" during the standoff.

Wong, during a visit to the headquarters of the Philippine Coast Guard, also announced a support package for the agency totaling 10 million Australian dollars ($6.7 million) for drone technology.

The US State Department also issued a statement criticizing China's actions, saying its "troubling pattern of provocations against the Philippines' legitimate maritime operations undermines regional peace and stability."
